mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead, making it ideal for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to a directory in your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a cluster named 'kagenti-cluster' with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on the Cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys Kagenti on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.4.0-alpha.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies autoscaling configuration.
> Applies monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, performing the upgrade, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.4.0-alpha.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

```

> Check the status of running pods before upgrading.
> Upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart version may not be compatible with your Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using a compatible Helm chart version with your Kubernetes version.

**Affected versions:** 1.30

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kagenti' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust resource requests/limits in your deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release with core functionalities for deploying AI agents, including lifecycle orchestration and security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this initial release.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti' showing all pods in a 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
